title: Codex Live IPC Injection - advance safely toward GUI auto-inject
constraints: Least danger/impact to the live Codex Desktop app; do not interfere/disable/break it.
No parallel/swarm contact with the live shared app-server router.
File-drop remains the proven default and fallback (Phase 0 shipped, GUI round-trip confirmed).
Never send any config/*, account/*, marketplace/*, plugin/*, or destructive thread method.
~/.codex/config.toml must be byte-identical before/after every live attempt.
Live real-thread writes require one explicit conversationId plus pre-send inspection; the original proof target was 019e932e-385b-7ee3-ad58-3157c9accaf5.

stories.title: IPC scaffold integrity & fail-closed safety gates verified
acceptanceCriteria: All scripts/codex_ipc_*.mjs pass `node --check`.
codex_ipc_client.mjs dry-run emits a uint32le `thread-follower-start-turn` envelope with explicit conversationId and minimal text input.
codex_ipc_client.mjs `--send` without `--ack-live-write` exits non-zero.
codex_ipc_client.mjs `--send --ack-live-write` with a non-authorized thread id exits non-zero.

stories.title: Negative owner-discovery probe: is the follower route externally reachable?
acceptanceCriteria: A fixed synthetic sentinel UUID is set internally (no external thread id accepted) and verified absent from ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite before any send.
Exactly one `thread-follower-start-turn` is sent to the live router with the sentinel conversationId; the router's response (or rejection) is captured verbatim.
~/.codex/config.toml is byte-identical before and after the probe.
A decision is recorded in the plan: follower route is externally VIABLE (router processed it, owner-not-found) or BLOCKED (router rejected/ignored external follower requests).

stories.title: Decision gate + conditional controlled real-thread write
stories.result: VIABLE+PROVEN+GUI-CONFIRMED: external thread-follower-start-turn forwarded by router to owning renderer; turn ran; model replied (IPCINJECTMARKER7F3A9 ACK). Isolation proven: global config sha unchanged, unexpectedNonTargetChangedIds=[]. Finding: renderer DERIVES turn params, so external model/effort override is NOT honored (turn ran at thread xhigh, not requested medium) - Claude input cannot change model/reasoning. User later confirmed the sent messages appeared in the Codex Windows app.
acceptanceCriteria: If US-002 = VIABLE: with explicit user go-ahead, one follower-start-turn on 019e932e (default model/effort first) is sent; the turn appears in the Desktop GUI; a before/after state_5.sqlite + config snapshot proves isolation (config unchanged, no unexpected non-target thread row changed).
If US-002 = BLOCKED: documented as a fundamental architectural blocker (Desktop resists external prompt injection); no real-thread write is attempted; file-drop confirmed as the durable solution.
No forbidden method is ever sent; every live attempt is time-bounded and logs the exact JSON sent.

stories.title: Productionize --ipc OR finalize file-drop
stories.result: PRODUCTIONIZED: handoff_to_codex.sh gains opt-in `--ipc <conversationId> "task"` that writes the file-drop then injects the pickup line into the live thread via codex_ipc_client.mjs (--allow-any-thread --send --ack-live-write), falling back to file-drop on any non-zero exit. File-drop remains default. End-to-end validated on test thread: model read for-codex.md and replied IPCPRODTEST ACK; config unchanged. Error paths (missing cid/task) exit 1.
acceptanceCriteria: If US-003 real write proven: handoff_to_codex.sh gains an opt-in `--ipc` mode that calls the Node client and falls back to file-drop on any non-zero exit; file-drop stays the default.
If US-003 blocked: handoff_to_codex.sh is unchanged; README/skill state the IPC status plainly.
`bash -n scripts/handoff_to_codex.sh` passes; `.\scripts\agent-context-check.ps1` ok.

stories.title: Docs synced + reviewer sign-off
stories.result: Plan progress/decision logs updated; README + skill document --ipc (marked experimental because of undocumented Desktop IPC internals, not because GUI confirmation is pending). code-reviewer verdict: APPROVE (0 Critical/0 High; 3 Medium + 3 Low addressed: stale comment, usage() flag, quoted path, experimental note). Fail-closed gates re-verified post-fix.
acceptanceCriteria: plans/2026-06-04-codex-ipc-injection.md progress + decision logs updated with this run's evidence.
state/agent-inbox/README.md and .claude/skills/handoff-to-codex accurately reflect current IPC status.
Reviewer verifies the above against fresh evidence (config invariant, captured router responses).

stories.title: Post-update IPC revalidation gate
stories.result: ADDED: scripts/codex_ipc_revalidate.mjs performs validate-only post-update checks for required files, Node syntax, Git Bash shell syntax, node:sqlite, Codex state files, named-pipe presence, optional target inspection, and optional read-only router initialize. Current runtime-read-probe passed with router initialize only; no prompt/follower-start-turn/config write/SQLite write/artifact generation.
acceptanceCriteria: The revalidation command can run without sending IPC prompts or generating artifacts.
A gated live read probe sends only router initialize and reports structured success/failure.
Missing optional Codex CLI version capture does not block IPC revalidation when state files, pipe presence, syntax, and read-only router initialize are healthy.

stories.title: No-UUID /ipc thread discovery
stories.result: ADDED: scripts/codex_ipc_thread_locator.mjs reads the Codex Desktop thread index read-only and filters candidate conversationIds by cwd, project, title text, and creation/update time. It found the known land_diligence Await message thread as exactly one candidate with --require-single. Candidate output is not write authority; selected ids must still be inspected before --ipc.
acceptanceCriteria: The locator sends no IPC messages and writes no SQLite files.
The locator can list known Codex Desktop projects and narrow a known thread to exactly one candidate.
The Claude /ipc skill instructs agents to inspect the selected candidate before sending.

stories.title: Static IPC contract audit
stories.result: ADDED: scripts/codex_ipc_contract_audit.mjs reads repo artifacts only and emits a requirement matrix. Current run reports 11/11 IPC contract requirements evidenced, with caveats that runtime proof must use revalidation and controlled live-write proof must use the dry-run-first harness.
acceptanceCriteria: The audit command sends no IPC messages, reads no SQLite runtime state, and writes no artifacts.
The audit covers default file-drop, opt-in IPC, explicit targeting, fallback, transcript pointers, inspect-before-send, no-UUID discovery, update revalidation, forbidden writes, and audit evidence.
The audit exits non-zero if a required file or requirement evidence is missing.

stories.title: Controlled write re-proof harness
stories.result: ADDED: scripts/codex_ipc_write_proof.mjs provides a dry-run-first future write re-proof workflow. Dry-run inspected the authorized test thread and emitted the full inspect/revalidate/snapshot/send/poll/compare plan without sending a prompt. Live mode requires --send --ack-live-write, uses one explicit conversationId, revalidates the runtime, sends one marker task through the maintained client, polls rollout JSONL for marker acknowledgement plus task_complete, and compares config/thread isolation in memory.
acceptanceCriteria: Dry-run sends no prompt and still inspects the target thread before presenting the live proof sequence.
Live mode requires --send and --ack-live-write, and non-test explicit UUID targets still require --allow-any-thread.
The harness fails closed on missing/archived/mid-turn target evidence, revalidation failure, send failure, missing marker acknowledgement, or isolation compare failure.
